message:
Sync beads: close caam-ldxo.5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -335,7 +335,7 @@
 {"id":"caam-ldxo.2","title":"Task: Harden install.sh (integrity + gum UX + cache-busting)","description":"Goal
- Make the installer safer, clearer, and more reliable while remaining a copy/paste one-liner.

Background
- Local installers (repo_updater, xf) show best practices: cache-busting, gum-enhanced output, non-interactive mode, and checksum verification.
- We need parity for caam with explicit integrity checks and predictable logging.

Details
- Add checksum verification + signature validation (from caam-ldxo.1).
- Add cache-busting query param in URLs (opt-out env var).
- Add gum-based formatting if gum is installed and stdout is a TTY; graceful fallback otherwise.
- Add NO_COLOR + CI/non-interactive behavior (no prompts, deterministic exit codes).
- Add explicit --verify option (run caam --version and optional self-test).
- Add explicit --channel=stable|beta and --version override.
- Ensure install is idempotent: if the same version is installed, skip download unless --force.
- Preserve previous binary as backup and restore if replace fails.
- Improve error messages with actionable guidance (missing curl/wget, PATH, permissions).

Tests
- Shell tests for: checksum failure, signature failure, gum present/absent, non-tty, cache-bust on/off, version already installed, rollback on partial failure.
- Mock GitHub API responses to validate parsing and fallback behavior.

Success
- Installer is safe, audit-friendly, and pleasant to read in the terminal.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:18.723957303Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T21:53:09.289837881Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.2","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:18.725403277Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.2","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:51:19.701293431Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-ldxo.3","title":"Task: Self-update command (caam update)","description":"Goal
- Provide an in-product, idempotent update path that verifies integrity and supports rollback.

Details
- Add `caam update` (and `caam update --check`) to fetch latest release metadata.
- Verify SHA256 + signature before replacing binary.
- Use atomic replace with temp file + rename; keep last-known-good binary for rollback.
- Support channels (stable/beta) and explicit version pinning.
- Emit structured logs (version_from, version_to, download_url, checksum_ok, signature_ok).
- Honor non-interactive mode (CI) with clear exit codes for "up-to-date", "updated", "failed".

Tests
- Unit tests for release metadata parsing and version comparison.
- Integration test with a fake local release server and bad checksum/signature cases.
- E2E test that verifies rollback restores previous version on failure.

Success
- Users can upgrade safely without re-running curl | bash.
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:25.027082534Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T19:10:22.331047703Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.3","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:25.028567021Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.3","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:51:31.725586041Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-ldxo.4","title":"Task: Auto-update scheduler + distributed fleet upgrade","description":"Goal
- Offer opt-in background update checks and coordinated upgrades across remote coordinators.

Details
- Add a scheduler (daemon/cron helper) to run `caam update --check` daily and surface notifications in TUI/CLI.
- For distributed auth recovery, provide `caam update --remotes` to update coordinator binaries via SSH/SFTP with checksum verification.
- Maintain idempotency: skip if versions match, and provide a --dry-run report.
- Add a rollback command to revert remote nodes to last-known-good.
- Ensure update checks are opt-in by default and never auto-apply without explicit consent.

Tests
- Dry-run snapshot tests for local + remote update plans.
- Mock SSH/SFTP tests to validate version detection, upgrade ordering, and rollback.
- Log validation tests to ensure structured logs include node, version, and outcome.

Success
- Users can keep single or multi-machine setups current with minimal friction.

-{"id":"caam-ldxo.5","title":"Task: Dependency bootstrap + doctor (gum/wezterm/tailscale/playwright)","description":"Goal
- Detect missing dependencies and offer safe, idempotent installation paths.

Background
- caam depends on optional tools (wezterm CLI, tailscale, gum, playwright/Chrome) for advanced features.
- Users should not have to guess what is missing; the tool should check and guide.

Details
- Add `caam doctor` (or extend existing diagnostics) to detect: gum, wezterm cli, tailscale, playwright, chrome/canary, jq, gh, ssh, systemd/launchd, node/pnpm.
- Provide OS-specific install suggestions; optionally run installs with --yes/--auto.
- For Playwright, detect Node and perform `playwright install` only when needed.
- Keep all installs idempotent and reversible; create backups where config edits are needed.
- Emit structured logs with dependency, status, recommendation, and install result.

Tests
- Mock PATH fixtures for missing/present tools.
- Verify non-interactive mode never prompts and returns actionable exit codes.
- E2E test that simulates a "missing deps" environment and validates the printed install plan.

Success
- Users can bring a machine into a fully working state with a single command.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:55.056920876Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-22T04:03:35.178624165Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.5","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:55.090025891Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"caam-ldxo.6","title":"Task: Installer + update E2E tests","description":"Goal
- Add automated E2E coverage for install.sh and caam update flows.

Details
- Use a hermetic test harness that simulates GitHub releases and local PATH.
- Validate: fresh install, idempotent re-install, checksum failure, signature failure, downgrade refusal, rollback success.
- Include gum-present and gum-absent cases.
- Emit structured logs per scenario with exit codes and paths touched.

Success
- Installer/update regressions are caught in CI before release.

 {"id":"caam-ljt","title":"Data retention and automatic cleanup","description":"## Purpose
Implement automatic cleanup of old activity logs to prevent unbounded database growth.

## Background
codex-pool uses configurable retention:
```go
logRetention: 7 * 24 * time.Hour, // Keep logs for 7 days
```

caam should have similar configurable retention with sensible defaults.

## Configuration
```yaml
# ~/.caam/config.yaml
analytics:
  retention_days: 90        # Keep detailed logs for 90 days
  aggregate_retention: 365  # Keep aggregates for 1 year
  cleanup_on_startup: true  # Run cleanup when caam starts
```

## Technical Implementation
```go
// internal/db/cleanup.go

// CleanupConfig holds retention settings
type CleanupConfig struct {
    DetailedRetentionDays   int
    AggregateRetentionDays  int
}

// DefaultCleanupConfig returns sensible defaults
func DefaultCleanupConfig() CleanupConfig {
    return CleanupConfig{
        DetailedRetentionDays:  90,
        AggregateRetentionDays: 365,
    }
}

// Cleanup removes old records
func (d *DB) Cleanup(cfg CleanupConfig) (deleted int, err error) {
    cutoff := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -cfg.DetailedRetentionDays)
    
    result, err := d.conn.Exec(`
        DELETE FROM activity_log 
        WHERE timestamp < ?
    `, cutoff)
    
    if err != nil {
        return 0, err
    }
    
    deleted, _ = result.RowsAffected()
    
    // Optionally VACUUM to reclaim space
    if deleted > 1000 {
        _, _ = d.conn.Exec("VACUUM")
    }
    
    return int(deleted), nil
}

// ScheduledCleanup runs cleanup periodically
func (d *DB) ScheduledCleanup(interval time.Duration, cfg CleanupConfig) {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
    for range ticker.C {
        d.Cleanup(cfg)
    }
}
```

### CLI Integration
```bash
# Manual cleanup
caam cleanup --dry-run
caam cleanup --older-than 30

# View database stats
caam db stats
Database: ~/.caam/data/caam.db
Size: 2.3 MB
Activity logs: 15,234 entries
Oldest entry: 2025-09-15
```

## Files to Create/Modify
- internal/db/cleanup.go
- internal/db/cleanup_test.go
- cmd/caam/cleanup.go (optional CLI command)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Old logs are deleted based on retention config
- [ ] Cleanup is idempotent and safe
- [ ] VACUUM runs after large deletions
- [ ] Dry-run mode shows what would be deleted
- [ ] Cleanup does not block normal operations
- [ ] Sensible defaults that work for most users","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:23:38.014094581Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T02:09:26.283091054Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T02:09:26.283091054Z","close_reason":"Implemented data retention and automatic cleanup: added cleanup.go with retention logic, cleanup_test.go with comprehensive tests, cleanup CLI command (caam cleanup with --dry-run/--days/--quiet), and integrated CleanupOnStartup into TUI. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ljt","depends_on_id":"caam-0gb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:32:09.546260125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-ljt","depends_on_id":"caam-fwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:29:12.362276729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-lkk1","title":"TUI: Add health.Storage to Model and initialize in New()","description":"Add health storage reference to TUI Model struct.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Add field: healthStorage *health.Storage to Model struct (model.go ~line 56)
2. In New() function, initialize: healthStorage: health.NewStorage('')
3. Ensure storage is available throughout Model methods

This is the foundation for all health data access in TUI.
